architecture.summary: FunPRM is a process reward model (PRM) for code generation that operates in two stages: (1) a Chain-of-Function prompting strategy guides the base LLM to generate modular code organized into functions, with each function treated as a reasoning step for PRM evaluation; (2) a meta-learning-based reward correction mechanism trains a lightweight correction table to denoise Monte Carlo-estimated partial-solution rewards using clean final-solution rewards obtained from unit-test evaluation, then uses gradient-based meta-optimization to refine the partial rewards such that the updated PRM generalizes better to clean final-solution data.

biblio.title: FunPRM: Function-as-Step Process Reward Model with Meta Reward Correction for Code Generation

claims.statement: FunPRM achieves state-of-the-art performance on LiveCodeBench with 80.9 pass@1 when combined with OpenAI O4-mini (High)

FunPRM outperforms baselines across all four base LLMs (Qwen3-30B-A3B, GPT-4o-mini, DeepSeek-Coder-33B, Qwen2.5-7B-Coder) and achieves best overall performance in 18 out of 20 settings on LiveCodeBench and BigCodeBench subsets
On LiveCodeBench (2025-02-01 present) with O4-mini (High), FunPRM achieves 100% on Easy (31 problems), 92.3% on Medium (39 problems), and 63.9% on Hard (61 problems)

FunPRM surpasses Self-Certainty baseline, indicating the necessity of a learned reward model compared to simpler heuristic-based test-time scaling strategies, and outperforms outcome reward model (ORM)-based scaling, highlighting the advantage of decomposing code generation into intermediate steps and assigning rewards at a finer granularity

In human evaluation on 100 problems where both FunPRM and baseline produce correct solutions and 100 where both produce incorrect solutions, FunPRM is consistently preferred over the base model (Qwen2.5-7B-Coder) for code readability (correct solutions) and reusability/ease of fixing (incorrect solutions)

FunPRM continues to improve the performance of the base LLM (Qwen2.5-7B-Coder) through test-time scaling on HumanEval+ and MBPP+ without additional training, demonstrating domain generalization capability

baseline.name: FunPRM w/o CoF: 80.1%, FunPRM w/o MRC: 80.2%

claims.statement: Ablation study on LiveCodeBench (2025-02-01 present) shows FunPRM full model achieves 80.9% overall, FunPRM without Chain-of-Function achieves 80.1%, and FunPRM without meta reward correction achieves 80.2%

As problem difficulty increases on LiveCodeBench, the distribution of the number of PRM steps (generated functions) generally increases, indicating harder problems typically require longer chains of logic, though some hard problems are solved using only a single function
